producer_note: Compañía Vinícola del Norte de España (CVNE), founded 1879 in Haro, is one of Rioja's most historic wineries, producing classic Rioja Alta wines including Imperial and Viña Real.
Matteo Correggia is a benchmark Roero producer in Canale d'Alba, founded in 1985, known for elevating Roero Nebbiolo and Barbera d'Alba to international acclaim.
Onson is a Thai craft spirits brand; limited public producer information available.
Tatratea is a Slovak tea-based herbal liqueur produced by Karloff in the Tatra Mountains region, launched in 2003.
Ohmine Shuzo is a sake brewery in Yamaguchi Prefecture, founded in 1822 and revived in 2010, known for premium junmai daiginjo using Yamada Nishiki rice.
Elizabeth Spencer Winery, founded in 1998 by Elizabeth Pressler and Spencer Graham, is based in Rutherford, Napa Valley, producing Cabernet Sauvignon and Sauvignon Blanc.
Sieur d'Arques is a cooperative founded in 1946 in Limoux, the leading producer of Crémant de Limoux and Blanquette de Limoux sparkling wines.
Domaine Faiveley, founded in 1825 in Nuits-Saint-Georges, is one of Burgundy's largest family-owned domaines with major Grand Cru holdings across the Côte de Nuits and Côte de Beaune.
Michel Lynch is a Bordeaux brand created by Jean-Michel Cazes of Château Lynch-Bages, producing approachable AOC Bordeaux wines from sourced grapes across the region.
Fermoy Estate, established in 1985, is a family-owned Margaret River winery in the Wilyabrup subregion, producing Cabernet Sauvignon, Chardonnay and Semillon-Sauvignon Blanc.
Domaine Derain, based in Saint-Aubin and run by Dominique Derain (and successor Julien Altaber), is a pioneering biodynamic Burgundy estate with vines in Saint-Aubin, Mercurey and Pommard.
Caol Ila is an Islay single malt distillery founded in 1846 near Port Askaig, owned by Diageo, known for lighter, smoky peated whiskies and a major component of Johnnie Walker blends.
Perfume Trees Gin is a craft gin brand inspired by Hong Kong botanicals; production is contracted in the Netherlands.
Viña J. Bouchon is a family winery founded in 1887 in Chile's Maule Valley, known for País and Cabernet Sauvignon, including the País Salvaje wild-vine project.
Massenez is an Alsatian eaux-de-vie and liqueur distillery founded in 1870 in Urmatt, renowned for fruit brandies such as Framboise and Poire Williams.
Provenance is an independent bottling range from Douglas Laing, releasing single cask single malts from across Scotland; this entry covers Lowland releases.
Morambro Creek is a family-owned winery in Padthaway, South Australia, producing Shiraz, Cabernet Sauvignon and Chardonnay from estate vineyards.
Grosset Wines, founded in 1981 by Jeffrey Grosset in Auburn, Clare Valley, is a benchmark Australian Riesling producer best known for Polish Hill and Springvale Rieslings.
Bianka and Daniel Schmitt run a biodynamic estate in Flörsheim-Dalsheim, Rheinhessen, certified Demeter, producing natural Riesling, Pinot Noir and Scheurebe.
U'Luvka is a Polish ultra-premium vodka produced from rye, wheat and barley, inspired by a 16th-century recipe from the court of King Stefan Batory.
Bodegas Matsu, owned by Vintae, is located in Toro, Spain, producing Tinta de Toro wines (El Pícaro, El Recio, El Viejo) from old-vine vineyards despite some labeling references.
Domaine Moreau-Naudet is a respected Chablis estate run by Virginie Moreau, farming around 12 hectares organically across Petit Chablis, Chablis, Premier and Grand Cru sites.
Four Pillars is a craft gin distillery founded in 2013 in Healesville, Yarra Valley, known for Rare Dry Gin, Bloody Shiraz Gin and Olive Leaf Gin.
Bindi Sergardi is a historic Tuscan family winery dating to 1349, with estates around Siena producing Chianti Classico, Chianti Colli Senesi and IGT Toscana wines.

Jacob's Creek is a major Australian wine brand owned by Pernod Ricard, founded in 1976 and named after a creek near the original Barossa Valley vineyard; the South Eastern Australia tier blends fruit from multiple regions.
Domaine Pierre Gaillard, founded in 1981 in Malleval, is a Northern Rhône estate producing Côte-Rôtie, Condrieu, Saint-Joseph and Cornas.
Jacob's Creek's Barossa Valley range, under Pernod Ricard, sources from the brand's historic home region for premium Shiraz and Cabernet labels such as Reserve and Double Barrel.
Brancott Estate, owned by Pernod Ricard, planted Marlborough's first commercial Sauvignon Blanc vines in 1973 and is among New Zealand's most influential producers.
Taiga Shtof is a Russian premium vodka brand inspired by Siberian taiga botanicals, distilled from grain and infused with cedar nuts and herbs.
Michel Couvreur is a négociant based in Bouze-lès-Beaune, Burgundy, who imports Scotch malt whisky and matures it in former sherry casks in Burgundian cellars.
Château Malartic-Lagravière is a Cru Classé de Graves estate in Léognan, Pessac-Léognan, owned by the Bonnie family since 1997, producing red and white classified growths.
Champagne Georges Laval is a small grower-producer in Cumières, certified organic since 1971 and farmed biodynamically by Vincent Laval, making low-dosage Premier Cru Champagnes.
Château Haut-Bages Libéral is a Fifth Growth (1855) Pauillac estate owned by the Villars-Lurton family, with vineyards adjacent to Latour producing Cabernet Sauvignon-led wines.
Castelli Estate is a winery in Denmark, in Western Australia's Great Southern region, producing cool-climate Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, Riesling and Cabernet Sauvignon.
Drumshanbo Gunpowder Irish Gin is produced by The Shed Distillery in Drumshanbo, County Leitrim, founded in 2014 and known for its gunpowder tea botanical.
Elderton Wines is a family-owned Barossa Valley estate based in Nuriootpa, founded in 1979 by Neil and Lorraine Ashmead, known for old-vine Command Shiraz.
Ferraris Agricola is a Piedmontese estate in Castagnole Monferrato, considered the leading specialist in Ruchè di Castagnole Monferrato DOCG.
Escarpment Vineyard, founded in 1999 by Larry McKenna in Martinborough, Wairarapa, is renowned for single-vineyard Pinot Noir and Chardonnay.
Bluecoat American Dry Gin is produced by Philadelphia Distilling, launched in 2006 as one of the first American craft gins, distilled from organic American grain.
Daishichi Sake Brewery, founded in 1752 in Nihonmatsu, Fukushima, is renowned for using the traditional kimoto method to produce premium junmai sake.
Château Fombrauge is a Grand Cru Classé estate in Saint-Christophe-des-Bardes, Saint-Émilion, owned by Bernard Magrez, with origins dating to the 16th century.
Château Cazauviel is a Bordeaux estate producing AOC Bordeaux wines; limited public information available.
Georges Laval is a grower Champagne house in Cumières; a Burgundy attribution is unlikely and likely a data error.
Bodega Renacer, founded in 2003 in Perdriel, Luján de Cuyo, Mendoza, is best known for its Punto Final Malbec and Enamore Amarone-style wines.
FashionTV-branded vodka is a licensed lifestyle product line produced in Poland.
Bodegas Protos, founded in 1927 in Peñafiel, was the founding winery of the Ribera del Duero DO and is known for Tempranillo crianza, reserva and gran reserva wines.
Casa Flamenco is a tequila brand from Mexico; limited public producer information available.
Aliénor is a small-production Napa Valley label producing Cabernet Sauvignon and Bordeaux-style blends.
888 Tres Ochos is a tequila brand produced in Jalisco, Mexico, from blue Weber agave.
Provenance is an independent bottling range from Douglas Laing offering single cask single malts; this entry covers Islands releases.
Kairos is a Marlborough wine label producing Sauvignon Blanc and other varieties from the Wairau and Awatere valleys.
Scrappy's Bitters is a craft cocktail bitters maker founded in 2008 in Seattle, Washington, producing small-batch bitters from whole spices, herbs and fruits.
Château Roc de Cambes is a Côtes de Bourg estate in Bourg-sur-Gironde owned by François Mitjavile of Tertre Roteboeuf, regarded as one of the appellation's finest wines.
Château Beauregard is a historic Pomerol estate dating to the 17th century, owned by the Moulin and Cathiard families, producing Merlot-dominant right-bank Bordeaux.
Maison Mirabeau, founded in 2010 by the Cronk family in Cotignac, is a Provence rosé specialist producing Côtes de Provence and IGP rosés.
Bob's Bitters is a craft cocktail bitters brand founded in London by Bob Petrie, producing a wide range of single-flavor bitters used in premium bars worldwide.
Champagne Huguenot-Tassin is a small grower-producer based in Celles-sur-Ource in the Côte des Bar (Aube), making Pinot Noir-led Champagnes from family vineyards.
Poderi Aldo Conterno, founded in 1969 in Bussia, Monforte d'Alba, is one of Barolo's most celebrated estates, producing Barolo Bussia, Romirasco, Cicala and Colonnello.
Plantation Rum (recently rebranded Planteray) is a négociant rum range by Maison Ferrand, sourcing rums from multiple Caribbean countries and finishing them in Cognac casks in France.
Spacewurz is a German herbal liqueur brand; limited public producer information available.
Rock Rose Gin is produced by Dunnet Bay Distillers, founded in 2014 in Caithness on Scotland's far north coast, using locally foraged botanicals including rhodiola rosea.
Fattoria Viticcio is a family-owned estate in Greve in Chianti, founded in 1964, producing Chianti Classico, Gran Selezione and Super Tuscan IGT wines.
Domaine Champalou, founded in 1983 by Catherine and Didier Champalou, is a benchmark Vouvray producer making dry, demi-sec, moelleux and sparkling Chenin Blanc.
Woodford Reserve is a Kentucky Straight Bourbon distilled at the historic Woodford Reserve Distillery in Versailles, owned by Brown-Forman, established in its modern form in 1996.
Bumbu Rum is produced in Panama and aged in the Caribbean, marketed as a craft rum inspired by 16th-century West Indian sailor recipes.
Michter's Distillery, revived in the 1990s and based in Louisville, Kentucky, produces small-batch and single-barrel American whiskeys including bourbon, rye and sour mash.
Tyrrell's Wines, founded in 1858 at Pokolbin, is one of Australia's oldest family-owned wineries and a Hunter Valley benchmark for Semillon and Shiraz, including the iconic Vat 1 and Vat 47.
Skyy Vodka is an American grain vodka launched in 1992 in San Francisco by Maurice Kanbar, now owned by Campari Group.
Domaine Lucien Crochet, based in Bué, is a leading Sancerre producer making Sauvignon Blanc and Pinot Noir from a mosaic of terroirs including the prestigious Le Chêne and La Croix du Roy cuvées.
Château Trottevieille is a Premier Grand Cru Classé B estate in Saint-Émilion owned by the Castéja family, with one of the appellation's oldest vineyards.
L'Acrobate is a French wine label; limited public producer information available.
Distinction Point is a Barossa Valley wine label producing Shiraz and Cabernet Sauvignon.
Carlos I is a premium Spanish brandy from Bodegas Osborne, aged using the solera system in Jerez, Andalusia, with origins dating to 1889.
Montelobos is an artisanal mezcal produced by maestro mezcalero Don Abel Lopez in Santiago Matatlán, Oaxaca, from organic Espadín agave.
Arietta Wines is a Napa Valley producer founded in 1996 by Fritz Hatton and John Kongsgaard, known for Bordeaux-style red blends and the white H Block from Hudson Vineyards.
Tenute Silvio Nardi is a Brunello di Montalcino producer founded in 1950, with Casale del Bosco and Manachiara estates producing single-vineyard Brunellos.
Filliers Distillery, founded in 1880 in Deinze, East Flanders, is a Belgian family distillery producing genever, Dry Gin 28 and single malt whisky.
Napa Cellars is a Napa Valley winery in Oakville, founded in 1976, producing Cabernet Sauvignon, Chardonnay, Merlot and Zinfandel.
Finlandia Vodka is produced in Koskenkorva, Finland, from Finnish six-row barley and glacial spring water; launched in 1970.
Domaine Duseigneur is a biodynamic Southern Rhône estate in Saint-Laurent-des-Arbres, producing Lirac, Châteauneuf-du-Pape and Côtes du Rhône wines.
Domaine Alphonse Mellot, based in Sancerre and dating back to 1513, is a leading biodynamic Sancerre producer renowned for La Moussière and Génération XIX cuvées.
Domaine Henry Pellé, based in Morogues, is the leading producer of Menetou-Salon AOC, making Sauvignon Blanc and Pinot Noir from limestone-rich terroirs.
Edinburgh Gin is produced in Edinburgh, Scotland, founded in 2010, known for its London Dry, Cannonball and a range of liqueurs and flavoured gins.
Ultimat Vodka is a Polish luxury vodka distilled from a blend of rye, wheat and potatoes, presented in a distinctive crystal decanter.
Holy Grass Vodka is produced by Dunnet Bay Distillers in Caithness, in Scotland's far north Highlands, infused with locally hand-picked holy grass (sweetgrass).
Château Pouget is a Fourth Growth (1855) Margaux estate in Cantenac, owned by the Guillemet family, producing classic Cabernet Sauvignon-led Margaux wines.
Maison Telmont, founded in 1912 in Damery, Vallée de la Marne, is committed to organic viticulture and is majority-owned by Rémy Cointreau and the Heyck family.
Glenmorangie Distillery, founded in 1843 in Tain, Ross-shire, is a Northern Highland single malt distillery owned by Moët Hennessy, famed for its tall stills and cask-finished whiskies.
Ilegal Mezcal is a small-batch artisanal mezcal produced in Tlacolula, Oaxaca, founded in 2004 from Espadín agave, now owned by Bacardi.
Château Minuty is a Cru Classé Côtes de Provence estate near Saint-Tropez, founded in 1936 by the Matton family, specializing in premium Provence rosé.
Gosling's, founded in 1806 in Bermuda, is the producer of Black Seal rum, the key ingredient in the Dark 'n' Stormy cocktail.

Marie Brizard is a historic French liqueur house founded in 1755 in Bordeaux, producing anisette, fruit liqueurs and a wide spirits range; the Bordeaux tag refers to its headquarters rather than wine production.
Château La Renaissance is a small Bordeaux estate (often associated with Pomerol-area Lalande-de-Pomerol bottlings); details vary by producer using the same name.
Champagne Charles Collin is a cooperative-based grower house in Fontette, Côte des Bar (Aube), producing Pinot Noir-led Champagnes including Cuvée Charles Collin and rosé.
Tenuta Luisa is a family winery in Mariano del Friuli, in the Friuli Isonzo DOC, producing Friulano, Sauvignon, Pinot Grigio and Bordeaux-style reds.
Domaine Georges Rafael is a Napa Valley winery producing Cabernet Sauvignon and Bordeaux-style blends.
